title: MemoryCapsule persistence via KernelActionCatalogV1 + WriteBoxV1
goal: Persist MemoryCapsule audit records through the existing KernelActionCatalogV1 + WriteBoxV1 mutation path (per packet forbidden_paths: 'No mutations through the backend inspector plane') so capsule history is durable, replayable, and review-gated. The persisted shape is the CapsuleAuditLog plus capsule metadata (not the full MemoryPack item contents, which already live in FEMS).
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/persistence.rs (CapsuleRecorder + CapsuleRecord typed shape) defining a KernelActionCatalogV1 action variant `CapsuleAuditRecord`. Wiring uses the existing kernel/write_box surface; no new mutation path. Tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/memory_persistence_tests.rs using a mock KernelActionCatalogV1.
unblock_conditions: MT-141/142/143 ship the capsule primitives + builder. KernelActionCatalogV1 + WriteBoxV1 already exist in main from KERNEL-002.
implementation_notes: Per the WP-KERNEL-004 packet forbidden_paths the ONLY mutation paths are KernelActionCatalogV1 + WriteBoxV1; no parallel mutation. CapsuleRecorder must therefore submit a typed action via the existing catalog, not invent a new write path. Surface: `pub struct CapsuleRecorder<'a> { action_catalog: &'a dyn KernelActionSubmitter }` with `pub fn record(record: CapsuleRecord) -> Result<RecordReceipt, RecorderError>`. CapsuleRecord shape: `{ capsule_id: Uuid (v7), capsule_source_hash: String, task_type: TaskType, policy: RetrievalPolicy, audit_log: CapsuleAuditLog, built_at_utc, recorded_at_utc, session_id, role_id, outcome: Option<CapsuleOutcome> }` where CapsuleOutcome is filled later by the outcome feedback loop (MT-158). RecordReceipt: `{ record_id: Uuid v7, write_box_envelope_id: Uuid v7, persisted_at_utc }`. Use serde_json for envelope payload; the catalog routes to durable storage via the existing WriteBoxV1 review path (review-gated per FEMS V1 invariant). Tests: recorder constructs a well-formed catalog action; record receipt round-trips; recorder fails cleanly on KernelActionCatalogV1 rejection with typed RecorderError; outcome field defaults to None at initial record.
risk_if_missed: Without persistence via the canonical mutation path, capsule audit history fragments across ad-hoc stores and the no-parallel-mutation invariant is violated.
validator_focus: All writes flow through KernelActionCatalogV1; no direct database calls. CapsuleRecord captures the full audit log so replay is possible without re-running retrieval.

minimum_controls: All persistence flows through KernelActionCatalogV1 + WriteBoxV1; no parallel mutation path.
CapsuleRecord captures the full audit log so replay does not depend on re-running retrieval.
RecorderError is typed so call sites cannot silently swallow persistence failures.
